---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*. 


Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Ctrl+Alt+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Ctrl+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
```



```{r}
library(readxl)
gaming_data <- gaming_mental_health_10M_40features_csv
glimpse(gaming_data)
read.csv("gaming_mentalhealth_10M_40features.csv") # we could not get read.csv to work so we will try again at a later time. For now we brute forced it with a manual import.

```
```{r}
#cleaned it a bit to make my computer run more smoothly
gaming_data %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.))))

summary(gaming_data$Hours_Played)
summary(gaming_data$Addiction_Level)

gaming_data %>%
  summarise(duplicates = sum(duplicated(.)))

#make my sample smaller
gaming <- gaming_data %>%
  sample_n(5000)
```

```{r}
#Checking addiction levels against hours played 
ggplot(gaming_data, aes(x = daily_gaming_hours, y = addiction_level)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "blue") +
  labs(
    title = "Addiction Level vs Hours Played",
    x = "Hours Played",
    y = "Self-Reported Addiction Level"
  )

ggplot(gaming, aes(x = daily_gaming_hours, y = addiction_level)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "blue") +
  labs(
    title = "Addiction Level vs Hours Played",
    x = "Hours Played",
    y = "Self-Reported Addiction Level"
  )
```

```{r}
# checking the correlation 
cor(gaming_data$daily_gaming_hours, gaming_data$addiction_level, use = "complete.obs")
cor(gaming$daily_gaming_hours, gaming$addiction_level, use = "complete.obs")

```

```{r}
#checking "liars"
gaming_data <- gaming_data %>%
  mutate(
    suspicious = case_when(
      daily_gaming_hours > quantile(daily_gaming_hours, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE) &
      addiction_level < quantile(addiction_level, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Underreporting",

      daily_gaming_hours < quantile(daily_gaming_hours, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE) &
      addiction_level > quantile(addiction_level, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Overreporting",

      TRUE ~ "Consistent"
    )
  )
gaming_data %>%
  count(suspicious)

```
```{r}
ggplot(gaming_data, aes(x = daily_gaming_hours, y = addiction_level, color = suspicious)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Identifying Inconsistent Addiction Reporting",
    x = "Daily Hours Played",
    y = "Addiction Level",
    color = "Classification"
  )
```

```{r}
gaming_data <- gaming_data %>%
  mutate(
    mental_health_score = (stress_level + anxiety_score + depression_score) / 3
  )
```

```{r}
ggplot(gaming_data, aes(x = daily_gaming_hours, y = mental_health_score)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Daily Gaming Hours vs Mental Health",
    x = "Daily Gaming Hours",
    y = "Mental Health Score"
  )
```

```{r}
cor(gaming_data$daily_gaming_hours, gaming_data$mental_health_score, use = "complete.obs")
```

```{r}
# Stress
ggplot(gaming_data, aes(x = daily_gaming_hours, y = stress_level)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)

# Anxiety
ggplot(gaming_data, aes(x = daily_gaming_hours, y = anxiety_score)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)

# Depression
ggplot(gaming_data, aes(x = daily_gaming_hours, y = depression_score)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)
```
```{r}
gaming_data <- gaming_data %>%
  mutate(
    gaming_group = case_when(
      daily_gaming_hours < 2 ~ "Low",
      daily_gaming_hours < 5 ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ "High"
    )
  )

gaming_data %>%
  group_by(gaming_group) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_stress = mean(stress_level, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_anxiety = mean(anxiety_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_depression = mean(depression_score, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
ggplot(gaming_data, aes(x = gaming_group, y = mental_health_score, fill = gaming_group)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Mental Health by Gaming Level",
    x = "Gaming Level",
    y = "Mental Health Score"
  )
```
```{r}
physical_data <- gaming_data %>%
  select(daily_gaming_hours, bmi, exercise_hours, eye_strain_score, back_pain_score) %>%
  drop_na()

physical_data <- physical_data %>%
  mutate(
    physical_health_score = (bmi + eye_strain_score + back_pain_score) / 3
  )
```

```{r}
ggplot(physical_data, aes(x = daily_gaming_hours, y = bmi)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Gaming Hours vs BMI")
ggplot(physical_data, aes(x = daily_gaming_hours, y = exercise_hours)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Gaming Hours vs Exercise Hours")
ggplot(physical_data, aes(x = daily_gaming_hours, y = eye_strain_score)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Gaming Hours vs Eye Strain")
ggplot(physical_data, aes(x = daily_gaming_hours, y = back_pain_score)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Gaming Hours vs Back Pain")

```
```{r}
cor(physical_data$daily_gaming_hours, physical_data$bmi)
cor(physical_data$daily_gaming_hours, physical_data$exercise_hours)
cor(physical_data$daily_gaming_hours, physical_data$eye_strain_score)
cor(physical_data$daily_gaming_hours, physical_data$back_pain_score)
```

```{r}
physical_data <- physical_data %>%
  mutate(
    gaming_group = case_when(
      daily_gaming_hours < 2 ~ "Low",
      daily_gaming_hours < 5 ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ "High"
    )
  )
physical_data %>%
  group_by(gaming_group) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_bmi = mean(bmi),
    avg_exercise = mean(exercise_hours),
    avg_eye_strain = mean(eye_strain_score),
    avg_back_pain = mean(back_pain_score)
  )
ggplot(physical_data, aes(x = gaming_group, y = eye_strain_score, fill = gaming_group)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Eye Strain by Gaming Level")
```

From here I will lower the sample size and see if it affects the charts
```{r}

ggplot(gaming, aes(x = daily_gaming_hours, y = addiction_level)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "blue") +
  labs(
    title = "Addiction Level vs Hours Played",
    x = "Hours Played",
    y = "Self-Reported Addiction Level"
  )
```
```{r}
gaming <- gaming %>%
  mutate(
    suspicious = case_when(
      daily_gaming_hours > quantile(daily_gaming_hours, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE) &
      addiction_level < quantile(addiction_level, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Underreporting",

      daily_gaming_hours < quantile(daily_gaming_hours, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE) &
      addiction_level > quantile(addiction_level, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Overreporting",

      TRUE ~ "Consistent"
    )
  )
gaming %>%
  count(suspicious)
```
